Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents a company using the LegalEase AI platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Company entity.

incorporationDate.description: The date the company was incorporated.

formationState.description: The state in which the company was formed.

ComplianceDate.title: ComplianceDate
ComplianceDate.description: Represents a compliance date for a company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ComplianceDate entity.

dueDate.description: The date the compliance task is due.

completedDate.description: The date the compliance task was completed.

description.description: A description of the compliance task.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a company document stored in the cloud.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

uploadDate.description: The date the document was uploaded.

fileUrl.description: The URL of the document in cloud storage.

BankAccount.title: BankAccount
BankAccount.description: Represents a bank account connected to the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BankAccount entity.

accountName.description: The name of the bank account.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction from a connected bank account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.
Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.
Unique identifier for the ChartOfAccounts entity.

accountType.description: The type of the account (e.g., asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the LegalEase AI platform.  Authentication data is not included in this entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

QuickBooksConnection.title: QuickBooksConnection
QuickBooksConnection.description: Stores OAuth 2.0 tokens for a user's QuickBooks integration.

id.description: Unique identifier, typically the user's UID.

connectedAt.description: The timestamp when the connection was established.

status.description: The current status of the connection.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  User ID is derived from Firebase Auth.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores company profiles owned by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

params.name: complianceDateId

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, DBAC, and support secure list operations (QAPs). User-owned data is structured under the `/users/{userId}` path to leverage path-based authorization. Company data and its related subcollections are structured under `/users/{userId}/companies/{companyId}` to maintain a clear ownership hierarchy. This approach allows security rules to be written based on the path and avoids complex `get()` calls.  Specifically, data belonging to a company (compliance dates, documents, bank accounts, invoices, chart of accounts) is nested under the company document. This ensures that access to these subcollections is controlled by ownership of the parent company document, achieved via path-based rules.

Since the `Company` entity includes a `userId` property, we leverage path based rules that check if `request.auth.uid == userId` to ensure proper access control.

QAPs are supported because all documents within a collection share the same security requirements. List operations are secured using rules that check ownership based on the path and the `userId` field in the documents.
